invariant_definition: One who has attained liberation while still embodied. The doctrine is principally Advaita; in Advaita the *jīvanmukta* lives out *prārabdha-karma* in a body whose phenomenality has ceased to bind. *Jīvanmukti* is rejected as incoherent by Madhva (true *mokṣa* requires the dropping of the *karma*-body), and is sharply qualified by Viśiṣṭādvaita (the *prapanna* lives in unbroken devotion but is technically not 'liberated' until shedding the body and attaining Vaikuṇṭha).

per_school.definition: Central doctrine. The *jīvanmukta* is the *brahma-jñāni* whose *avidyā* has been removed but whose body persists due to *prārabdha-karma*. He acts spontaneously, without ego-arrogated agency, in continuing conformity with *dharma*. *Naiṣkarmya-Siddhi* and *Pañcadaśī* develop the doctrine systematically.
*Jīvanmukti* in the strong Advaita sense (final liberation while embodied) is rejected. The *prapanna* lives in unbroken devotion but final *mokṣa* (Vaikuṇṭha-attainment) is *videha* — post-mortem.
Rejected. *Mokṣa* requires shedding the *karma*-body and ascending to Vaikuṇṭha; *jīvanmukti* is incoherent.

translator_note: 'Living-liberated one' or 'liberated-while-living' both acceptable. The category is structurally central to Advaita and structurally absent from Mādhva Vedānta — this is one of the cleanest school-distinctions.
